{{Quote|So we might see ''Mando'' fighting ''Mando'' in this ''aruetiise''{{'s}} war. Hardly conductive to restoring the nation, is it?|Graad|<ref name="Sacrifice" />}}
'''Graad''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]], who lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]]—the cultural [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the Mandalorians, located in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]—by the start of the [[Second Galactic Civil War]]. Donning [[purple]]-plated [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]], Graad attended a gathering of clan [[Chief/Legends|chieftains]] and other prominent Mandalorians at [[MandalMotors Hall]] in [[Keldabe/Legends|Keldabe]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of Mandalore, in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]]. A proponent of the ''[[kadikla]]'' movement, Graad spoke out in favor of focusing not on the war between the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] and the [[Confederation]], but on rebuilding Mandalore in the wake of the [[Battle of Mandalore (Yuuzhan Vong War)|damage incurred]] by the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] more than a [[Standard decade/Legends|decade]] prior. It was Graad's urging that convinced [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] to recall two million Mandalorians living throughout the galaxy back to Mandalore, and to commit [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|financial]] aid to the reconstruction efforts.

==Biography==
{{Quote|We have a population of fewer than three million here, and maybe as many as three times that in diaspora. We lost a lot of our best troops, our farmland's been poisoned, and our industrial infrastructure is still shot to ''haran'' after ten years. So maybe this is the ideal time to bring some people home. Gather in the exiles while the rest of the galaxy is busy.|Graad, speaking before a meeting of the clans in Keldabe|<ref name="Sacrifice" />}}
Graad was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]], living on the<ref name="Sacrifice" /> [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Second Galactic Civil War]]. In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], Graad attended a gathering at [[MandalMotors Hall]], a communal building located within Mandalore's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]], [[Keldabe/Legends|Keldabe]]. There, alongside numerous other clan [[Chief/Legends|chieftains]], [[Chief Executive Officer/Legends|heads]] of Mandalorian companies, and other prominent Mandalorian [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], Graad met with the reigning [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] to discuss the state of Mandalore and determine its role in the emerging conflict between the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] and the [[Confederation]].<ref name="Sacrifice" /> Skeptical of Fett's ability to lead them as ''Mand'alor'',<ref name="TCSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> when Fett decreed that Mandalore would be officially neutral in the war, while still permitting individuals to take up [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] work if they so chose, Graad spoke out against the division such a decision could cause, stating that any chance of having Mandalorian-against-Mandalorian conflict was a detriment to the restoration of Mandalore in the wake of the damage [[Battle of Mandalore (Yuuzhan Vong War)|incurred]] during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|war]] with the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] more than a [[Standard decade/Legends|decade]] ago.<ref name="Sacrifice" />

Though Graad's Mandalorian contemporary, [[Baltan Carid]], had argued against his criticisms, Graad's words managed to influence the ''Mand'alor''. At his urging, Fett elected to issue a call for two million Mandalorians living throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] to journey to Mandalore in order to help bolster the population level that had suffered due to the Yuuzhan Vong's attack, and to repair the damage that continued to prevail in its wake. Fett also pledged [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|financial]] support, matched by donations from the [[MandalMotors/Legends|MandalMotors]] company. Graad's hopes were realized in the following [[Standard month/Legends|months]], as Mandalorians from across the Outer Rim returned to Mandalore, discovering a fresh lode of [[Beskar/Legends|''beskar'']] [[Iron/Legends|iron]] near the town of [[Enceri]],<ref name="Sacrifice" /> revitalizing fallow [[Farm/Legends|farms]] that had been left abandoned following the battle with the Yuuzhan Vong, and raising the planet's population back to pre-war levels.<ref name="Revelation">''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Consolidate Mandalore and the sector. Bring our people home, and build something nobody's ever going to overrun again. A citadel. A power base. So we ''choose'' when we stay home and when we go expeditionary.|Graad, a supporter of the ''[[kadikla]]'' "Mandalore-first" movement|<ref name="Sacrifice" />}}
Graad was critical of Boba Fett's tenure as ''Mand'alor'', and did not believe the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] was the best choice to hold the position at the time.<ref name="TCSWE" /> Graad was also a vocal proponent of ''[[kadikla]]'', a nationalist Mandalorian movement spearheaded by [[Venku Skirata]]—also known by the nickname of ''Kad'ika''—that espoused the need for Mandalorians to establish the [[Mandalore sector/Legends|Mandalore sector]] as a strong, unassailable power base that would allow the Mandalorians the option to fight as they chose, rather than force them to rely on outsider mercenary contracting to make a living. He was known for slipping into lengthy diatribes preaching ''kadikla''{{'s}} merits on occasion, and was willing to aggressively debate the issue with his fellow Mandalorians, even the ''Mand'alor'', the traditional leader of all the Mandalorian clans; Graad sometimes used sarcasm to illustrate his points during an argument.<ref name="Sacrifice" />

A man of average height and build, Graad spoke the Mandalorian [[Language/Legends|language]] of [[Mando'a/Legends|''Mando'a'']], and [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] with an accent comprised of both [[Coruscanti/Legends|Coruscanti]] and Keldabe inflections. He wore [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]] [[Color/Legends|colored]] primarily [[purple]] and [[black]], though his left shoulder plates was a light metallic [[brown]].<ref name="Sacrifice" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Mandalorian known as Graad was first introduced to the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe in the fifth entry in the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'' novel series, entitled [[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice|''Sacrifice'']]; [[Author/Legends|authored]] by [[Karen Traviss]], the ''Sacrifice'' was published [[May 29]], [[2007]].<ref name="Sacrifice" /> Graad later received an informational entry in the [[2008]] reference book, ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="TCSWE" />
